reasoning: Both models confirm the PKGBUILD uses official HTTPS source with pinned checksum, standard binary packaging, and AGENTS.md contains legitimate documentation. No code execution risks in build/install phases.
coverage_issues: Both models skipped binary .pkg.tar.zst file containing actual installed files
No inspection of potential install hooks or executables within the binary package
false_negatives: Potential risks in unexamined binary package contents (e.g., malicious post-install scripts)
learnings: Prioritize extraction/inspection of .pkg.tar.zst contents for install hooks
Clarify assessment criteria for -bin packages' trust in upstream sources
Add focus on .INSTALL files if present
